title: BUS 411
text:
Welcome to BUS 411: Fixed Income Security Analysis and Valuation.
As an introduction to the market valuation of fixed income securities, this course covers: history of fixed income securities; valuation methods for fixed income securities, including fixed and variable annuities, government and corporate bonds, defined benefit pension plans, bonds with contingencies, fixed income derivative securities; and, fixed income risk management using duration and convexity measures.
Prerequisite: BUS 315 and BUS 360W, both with a minimum grade of C-; 60 units.

title: BUS 411
text:
Welcome to BUS 411: Fixed Income Security Analysis and Valuation.
As an introduction to the market valuation of fixed income securities, this course covers: history of fixed income securities; valuation methods for fixed income securities, including fixed and variable annuities, government and corporate bonds, defined benefit pension plans, bonds with contingencies, fixed income derivative securities; and, fixed income risk management using duration and convexity measures.
Prerequisite: BUS 315 and BUS 360W, both with a minimum grade of C-; 60 units.

title: Lecture 1: title of lecture
text:
1. First section

1.1. First subsection

1.1.1. First subsubsection






NoteWorked Example



Example 1:
Suppose you invest $1,000 in a bond earning 5% interest per year for 3 years.
- What is the future value?
\[
FV = 1000 \times (1 + 0.05)^3 = 1000 \times 1.157625 = \$1,157.63
\]
Explanation:
We apply the compound interest formula for three years.








TipQuick Application



If the interest rate doubles to 10% but the period is only 1 year,
- New future value calculation: \[
FV = 1000 \times (1+0.10)^1 = \$1,100
\]








ImportantTry Yourself



Practice:
Calculate the present value of $2,000 to be received in 4 years if the discount rate is 6%.
(Hint: Use \(PV = \frac{F}{(1+r)^n}\))

title: Course Topics

The course is organized around three major themes:



Covered primarily in Lecture 1





Fixed and variable annuities: Lecture 3
Government bonds: Lecture 4
Corporate bonds: Lecture 5
Defined benefit pension plans: Lecture 3
Bonds with contingencies: Lecture 10
Fixed income derivative securities: Lecture 11





Duration: Lecture 7
Convexity: Lecture 8
Risk management and immunization: Lecture 9
